scrutiny_review.summary: Feature successfully aligns documentation and regression tests with the hard-cut auth/bootstrap and tenant control-plane routes. All retired aliases are consistently removed from docs, and a new characterization test prevents drift.

observations.description: New regression test auth_and_tenant_control_docs_match_the_hard_cut_route_story() validates that canonical routes remain present and retired routes stay absent across 7 key documentation files
Test successfully executed and passed on current codebase
Documentation files consistently reflect canonical routes and remove retired aliases
Feature reinforces security posture by ensuring retired auth/bootstrap routes stay absent from documentation and preventing accidental re-introduction
observations.security_impact: Retired routes like /api/v1/auth/profile could have been exploited if frontend teams referenced stale docs; test now prevents this drift

observations.description: Control-plane routes correctly consolidate to /api/v1/superadmin/tenants/{id}/... pattern, maintaining tenant isolation boundaries
observations.security_impact: Prevents confusion between tenant-scoped admin paths and platform-wide superadmin control plane

observations.description: Characterization test guards against future documentation drift that could mislead frontend developers about available endpoints
All documentation files now consistently reference canonical routes and explicitly remove retired aliases
key_changes: Must-change-password corridor documentation updated to reference only GET /api/v1/auth/me (not /api/v1/auth/profile)
Frontend handoff docs remove profile endpoint table entries and update flow descriptions
Tenant control-plane docs consolidate to superadmin routes only
Auth hardening library aligns rollout caveats with current route story

observations.description: Feature includes both code-level test and documentation updates, following repository convention for docs-plus-test packets

non_blocking_issues.description: Characterization test uses string matching on documentation files rather than parsing structured OpenAPI schema
non_blocking_issues.impact: Test could miss nuanced documentation formats or false positives, though adequate for current markdown-based docs
non_blocking_issues.recommendation: Consider future enhancement to parse OpenAPI schema directly for API route assertions if documentation structure evolves
